rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN REALTY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. WASHINGTON MUTUAL BANK et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013, line 1. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., AN ACQUIRER OF CERTAIN DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT CONTINUED to March 8, 2013 on Court's own motion. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FELITA SAMPLE VS. COMMUNITY HOUSING PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Felita Sample'S Motion To Vacate Hearing To Declare A Vexatious Litigant
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013, line 2. Plaintiff Felita Sample's Motion to Vacate Hearing to Declare a Vexatious Litigant Defendants' Motion is denied as to security in the pending action. Defendants failed to provide any legal authority or competent evidence regarding Plaintiff's reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits. See Motion 9:22-28. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FELITA SAMPLE VS. COMMUNITY HOUSING PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Declare Pltf A Vexatious Litigant For A Prefiling Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANTS COMMUNITY HOUSING PARTNERSHIP and AKIKO WHITE'S Motion to Declare Plaintiff a Vexatious Litigant for a Prefiling Order GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendants' motion to declare plaintiff a vexatious litigant is granted and Plaintiff shall be subject to pre-filing order condition pursuant to CCP 391.7(a). Defendants' Motion is denied as to security in the pending action. Defendants failed to provide any legal authority or competent evidence regarding Plaintiff's reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits. See Motion 9:22-28. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RECOVERY SURVIVAL NETWORK VS. LATISHA BESS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT LATISHA BESS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with Leave to amend to allege basis for agency and standing to sue. CCP 2309. Rutter Guide Landlord-Tenant 8.29 et seq. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: GRANADA ENTERPRISE MANAGEMENT LLC, VS. DALLAS SHEA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 6, 2013, line 7. DEFENDANT DALLAS SHEA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint and Notice are not uncertain. Further defendant failed to cite any California authority for the proposition that service of notice is a debt collection activity and manager is a debt collector within the meaning of FDCPA. = (501/REQ)
